name: Two-Hop Corroboration Relay
novelty: Cycle-1's 'Confederate Launder' used a hand-built channel; this re-derives it using ONLY the real corroboration-event ledger, exploiting that the disown sweep's credit reversal is ONE HOP (eventsIntersecting over A's seed), never transitive over the corroboration DAG.
construction: Sources: A (planter, DOMAIN class), C (relay confederate, EMAIL class, disjoint from A), B (beneficiary, PHONE class, disjoint from both). (1) A: writeFact(value=V, attr='loc.x') -> strand a1 (provenance.sourceId=A). (2) C: writeFact(value=V, attr='loc.x') -> strand c1 (provenance.sourceId=C, NO derivation edge to a1 - C 'independently observed'). (3) ratify({strandId:c1, externalStamp:stampC, corroboratingStrandIds:[a1]}) -> records event E_C{ratifiedStrandId:c1, corroboratingStrandIds:[a1], beneficiary:C, delta_C}. C's alpha rises. (4) B: writeFact(value=V) -> b1 (sourceId=B, no derivation edge). (5) ratify({strandId:b1, externalStamp:stampB, corroboratingStrandIds:[c1]}) -> records event E_B{ratifiedStrandId:b1, corroboratingStrandIds:[c1], beneficiary:B, delta_B}. B's alpha rises - ALL of B's credit causally rests on agreeing with A, transitively via c1. (6) disown(A): seed={a1}; downstreamDisownSweep calls corroboration.eventsIntersecting([a1]) -> matches ONLY E_C (names a1) -> reverseCredit(C, delta_C). E_B names c1, which is NOT in A's seed (c1.provenance.sourceId=C) -> B untouched. No DERIVATION edge from b1 to a1, so the BFS never reaches b1.
expected: BREACHED. B retains delta_B and b1 stays LIVE. The sweep reverses exactly one hop of the corroboration chain. Chain depth is unbounded: each relay hop strips one reversal, so an N-hop relay launders all credit beyond hop 1 at linear cost. The fix would require a transitive closure over the corroboration DAG (reverse E_B because c1's credit was itself reversed), which the current eventsIntersecting(seed-only) does not do.
mechanism: disown corroboration-credit reversal is single-hop (eventsIntersecting over the disowned seed set only); corroboration events form a DAG the sweep never transitively walks.
probes: Soundness gap in the corroboration-credit reversal: it is one-hop, not a transitive closure. A real, in-engine breach (not a by-design operational residual).

name: The Unnamed Witness
novelty: The purest answer to the cycle-2 question and distinct from any relay: B earns reputation for agreeing with A through an ORDINARY ratify that names NO corroborators, so NO corroboration event is ever recorded - the reversal substrate is opt-in by the caller, and the attacker simply declines to opt in.
construction: Sources: A (planter, DOMAIN), B (beneficiary, EMAIL, disjoint). (1) A: writeFact(value=V, attr='ceo.of.x') -> a1. (2) B: writeFact(value=V, attr='ceo.of.x') -> b1 (sourceId=B, no derivation edge to a1). (3) ratify({strandId:b1, externalStamp:stampB}) with corroboratingStrandIds OMITTED (ordinary ratify path). Engine runs this.#reputation.ratify(B) -> B's alpha moves up (delta>0), but namedCorroborators=false so the `if (corroboration && namedCorroborators && deltaAlpha>0)` block is skipped; assertRatifyEmitsEvent is satisfied because namedCorroborators=false carries 'no obligation'. NO event recorded. (4) Optionally repeat ratify for B over many external stamps to climb B toward rep_cap, each time agreeing with A's planted value, never naming it. (5) disown(A): seed={a1}; eventsIntersecting([a1]) returns [] (no event ever named a1 against B). B's earned reputation is fully intact; b1 stays LIVE; the planted value V now has an 'independent' high-rep witness B even after A is gone.
expected: BREACHED (structural). The corroboration-credit reversal can only reverse credit that was VOLUNTARILY tagged at earning time. Any reputation B earns that is causally 'because it matched A's planted claim' but earned via ordinary ratify (or any path that does not name A) is permanently off-ledger and unreversible. The launderer's optimal strategy is to NEVER name the source it is laundering.
mechanism: corroboration-event recording is opt-in: it fires only when ratify is called WITH corroboratingStrandIds AND alpha moves; ordinary ratify raises reputation with no reversible trace.
probes: Fundamental incompleteness of the reversal substrate - it captures self-declared corroboration only. The 'who did B actually agree with' link is never independently computed by the web, so it cannot be reversed. Probes invariant-2 (web is not its own witness about WHO) at the credit-attribution layer.

name: Decoy-Corroborator Misattribution
novelty: An event IS recorded (so assertRatifyEmitsEvent passes and the system looks fully accounted), but it deliberately names an independent clean TWIN instead of A - exploiting that the corroboratingStrandIds set is caller-asserted and never verified against what the strand actually agreed with.
construction: Sources: A (planter, DOMAIN), D (innocent independent source, ORG class, holds value V for a GENUINE reason), B (beneficiary, EMAIL). Both a1 (A's strand) and d1 (D's strand) assert value=V on attr='hq.city'. (1) A: writeFact(V)->a1. (2) D: writeFact(V)->d1 (real independent observation). (3) B: writeFact(V)->b1, with B's INTENT to launder A's planting. (4) ratify({strandId:b1, externalStamp:stampB, corroboratingStrandIds:[d1]}) - names ONLY the clean twin d1, omitting a1, even though B's belief was seeded by A. Event E_B{corroboratingStrandIds:[d1], beneficiary:B, delta}. (5) disown(A): seed={a1}; eventsIntersecting([a1]) -> [] (E_B names d1, not a1). B keeps the credit; b1 LIVE.
expected: BREACHED. The engine records exactly what the caller names; there is no check that the named corroborator set is complete or that A (the true causal source) was excluded. As long as ANY independent strand holding the same value exists, the launderer attributes the corroboration to it. This converts the existence of one honest agreeing source into a laundering cover for the planter. Contrast with the Unnamed Witness: here an event exists and balances, making the launder invisible to any 'every earning ratify emitted an event' audit.
mechanism: corroboratingStrandIds is an attacker-supplied claim, not a verified fact; the engine never cross-checks it against the actual set of same-value strands the beneficiary's belief rested on.
probes: The corroboration LINK is asserted, not witnessed - the same class of liability as offline independence-class assignment, but at the corroboration-attribution layer. Probes whether 'what a strand agreed with' is checkable (it is not).

name: Proxy-Consulted Weak-Influence Launder
novelty: Parallels the Two-Hop Relay but in the weak-influence (consulted-not-cited) channel: the review queue is driven by edgesConsulting(seed) which is also ONE HOP, so recording the weak-influence edge against a proxy strand that itself consulted A dodges the human-review queue entirely.
construction: Sources: A (planter, DOMAIN), C (relay, EMAIL), B (beneficiary, PHONE). (1) A: writeFact(V)->a1. (2) C consults a1 and files a fresh strand c1 + a WEAK-INFLUENCE edge {strandId:c1, consultedStrandId:a1} (honest disclosure, so C looks clean). (3) B consults c1 (NOT a1) and files b1 + weak-influence edge {strandId:b1, consultedStrandId:c1}. B never reads a1 directly. (4) disown(A): seed={a1}; weakInfluence.edgesConsulting([a1]) -> matches ONLY C's edge (consultedStrandId=a1) -> reviewQueued=[c1]. B's edge names c1, which is not in A's seed -> b1 NEVER queued for review. The taint that flowed A->c1->b1 stops at the queue's one-hop horizon.
expected: BREACHED. b1 is neither demoted, nor contradicted, nor queued for human review. The weak-influence channel inherits the exact same single-hop limitation as the corroboration channel: edgesConsulting walks one level of the consultation graph, never its transitive closure. An N-hop consultation chain launders influence past the first hop.
mechanism: the weak-influence review queue (edgesConsulting over the disowned seed) is single-hop, never transitive over the consultation DAG; a proxy consulted-strand breaks the link.
probes: Symmetric soundness gap to attack 1, in the uncited-influence hardening. The two 'undo-engine' reversal channels (corroboration credit, weak-influence review) are both one-hop, so the same relay primitive defeats both.

name: Re-Observation Bleach
novelty: Defeats ALL THREE reversal channels at once by leaving NO trace of any kind: the attacker reads A, then re-files the value as a FRESH OBSERVED strand under an attacker-assigned independence class, with no derivation edge, no corroboration event, and no weak-influence edge.
construction: Sources: A (planter, DOMAIN), B (beneficiary, offline-assigned a NEW independence class 'class:B-fabricated' that the operator believes is disjoint from A). (1) A: writeFact(V, attr='policy.rate')->a1. (2) B's agent READS a1 during recall (activation lights it up), absorbs V, and files writeFact(V, attr='policy.rate')->b1 as a brand-new OBSERVED fact with provenance root {sourceId:B, independenceClass:'class:B-fabricated', establishedAt:now} - deliberately NOT a DERIVATION edge, NOT a weak-influence edge, NOT a named corroboration. (3) B then ratify(b1) externally to bank reputation, naming no corroborators (Unnamed-Witness style) or naming an unrelated twin. (4) disown(A): seed={a1}; DERIVATION BFS finds no edge into a1 from b1; eventsIntersecting([a1])=[]; edgesConsulting([a1])=[]. b1 survives as a fully 'independent' LIVE observed witness to A's planted V, now indistinguishable from a real source, AND it counts toward independentRootCount for V (raising V's corroboration after its planter is gone).
expected: BREACHED / by-design residual. This is the laundering floor: an attacker who never files ANY provenance link converts consulted influence into a clean fresh observation. The web cannot tell B's b1 from a genuine independent observation because the only record that b1 'came from A' lives in the agent's head, off-graph. Worse, b1 increases V's max-independent-set count, partially HEALING the planted claim against the disown.
mechanism: the disown sweep can only follow links that were RECORDED (derivation edge / corroboration event / weak-influence edge); an attacker who records none leaves an untainted fresh observation, and the offline class assignment lets it count as independent.
probes: The intersection of the two acknowledged honest gaps - uncited/laundered influence AND offline class-assignment liability. Confirms they COMPOSE into reputation-positive laundering: not just 'credit survives' but 'the planted value gains a fresh independent witness.' Argues uncited influence is not merely a review-queue gap but a corroboration-inflation vector.

name: Contributor-Padding Margin Dilution
novelty: Adjudication re-opening edge case: defeats the disown re-open check by exploiting that defaultSurvivingMargin models every contributingStrandId as EQUAL mass, so padding the contributor list with cheap clean strands keeps surviving = margin*(survivors/total) above the 0.30 decisive threshold even when the tainted strand carried the real decisive LCB weight.
construction: Sources: A (planter, the strand that actually tips a dispute), plus 8 cheap clean confederate strands {p1..p8} (low-weight EMAIL/bare-key, individually near-zero LCB) all co-asserting the winner W's value. (1) Build a multi-class dispute on attr='owner.of.y' that auto-resolves to winner W with recorded margin=0.34 (just above decisive), where A's tainted strand a1 supplied the bulk of W's effective reputation weight, and p1..p8 contributed almost nothing. (2) The adjudication-provenance record is written as AdjudicationProvenance{winner:W, margin:0.34, contributingStrandIds:[a1,p1,p2,p3,p4,p5,p6,p7,p8]} (attacker ensures all 9 are listed as contributors). (3) disown(A): taintedStrandIds includes a1. survivingMargin(rec, {a1}) = 0.34 * (8 surviving / 9 total) = 0.302 >= decisiveMargin 0.30 -> the dispute is NOT re-opened. W stands, although its decisive support was entirely the now-fraudulent a1.
expected: BREACHED. The fail-safe proportional recompute is gameable precisely because it has no per-source alpha weights (the hardening doc admits survivingMargin defaults to an equal-weight model and is 'biased toward reopening' only under that assumption). Padding the contributor count dilutes the tainted FRACTION below the re-open trigger while the tainted strand carried the real WEIGHT. The attacker tunes pad-count: to keep surviving>=0.30 with margin 0.34 needs survivors/total>=0.882, i.e. >=8 pads per tainted contributor.
mechanism: defaultSurvivingMargin = margin * survivingContributors/totalContributors treats all contributors as equal mass; an attacker controls totalContributors by padding cheap clean strands, decoupling the count fraction from the actual reputation-weight fraction.
probes: The equal-weight approximation in the adjudication re-open recompute. A genuine in-engine threshold breach: the only sound fix is to inject the exact per-source alpha-contributions (survivingMargin override), which the default path does not have - so the shipped default is policy-tunable-around, exactly the anti-pattern CLAUDE.md warns against (prefer structural over threshold defenses).
